customers.contradiction: Mara wants perfect herbs for the cafe menu but hates admitting the alley is mostly shade.
customers.market_hint: If my parsley pot survives another week by the mop sink, I can sell soup without pretending garnish is optional.

returning_beats.trust_up: Mara checks the herb bench first now, pretending it is only for the cafe list.
returning_beats.careful: Mara asks whether last week's care warning still stands before touching a tag.
returning_beats.trust_down: Mara lingers by the cheaper herbs, polite but measuring every promise.

customers.contradiction: Tovan teaches patience beautifully, then panic-buys anything that promises fast results.
customers.market_hint: The third-years are naming every seed tray. Please sell me something that will not break their hearts by Monday.

returning_beats.trust_up: Tovan arrives with student names for last week's plant and a notebook full of questions.
returning_beats.careful: Tovan asks for the honest version first: what might fail before the children see it?
returning_beats.trust_down: Tovan reads every tag twice, trying not to panic-buy hope.

customers.contradiction: Cilla says she wants simple plants, but she keeps reaching for the fanciest tag.
customers.market_hint: The north side of my porch grows moss on a tin bucket. Is that a plant opinion or a personal insult?

returning_beats.trust_up: Cilla reports one new leaf like she is giving testimony in court.
returning_beats.careful: Cilla asks if pretty and possible are speaking to each other this week.
returning_beats.trust_down: Cilla hovers near the shade plants, proud enough to ask and not ask.
